# Series 08 — Axiom-Check Log

**The committed record of `#print axioms` over every Series 08 headline. Regenerated by building `Series08.AxiomCheck` (`series-08/formal/Series08/AxiomCheck.lean`). Every headline reduces to Mathlib's standard three — `propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound` — with no `sorry` and no custom axiom.**

*Generated 2026-07-11 from a clean `lake build Series08 Series08.AxiomCheck` (Lean 4 `v4.15.0` / Mathlib `v4.15.0`), and **re-verified live after each Phase E pass** (series-review-1 R3, series-review-2 R3, series-review-3 R2): every headline still prints exactly the standard three. A publication should cite the specific commit hash and a clean-build log.*

---

Each theorem below printed exactly `depends on axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.

## WS1 — the no-god's-eye spine (PARTIAL after series-review-3 S1: Impossibility-proved core; independent spine resists — coincides with relational identity)
- `Series08.WS1.ws1_gods_eye_collapses` — the positionless collapse (a theorem; scope: coincides with relational identity — the honest content is the Series 07 collapse)
- `Series08.WS1.ws1_symmetric_bisim_trivial` — the positionless node is label-bisimilar to the trivial self-loop
- `Series08.WS1.ws1_symmetric_states_bisimilar` — OWNS the coincidence: positionless ⟹ all states bisimilar (so the collapse IS relational identity)
- `Series08.WS1.ws1_symLoop_not_behav` — a ≥2-face symmetric node cannot host relationally-identified plurality
- `Series08.WS1.ws1_labelLoop_not_symmetric` — the surviving plural node is NOT symmetric (Failed condition does not arise)
- `Series08.WS1.ws1_no_gods_eye` — the recoverable-face collapse (Series 07 engine, retained)
- `Series08.WS1.ws1_no_gods_eye_node`
- `Series08.WS1.ws1_hold_forced`
- `Series08.WS1.ws1_symmetric_hold_recoverable`
- `Series08.WS1.ws1_directed_hold_free`
- `Series08.WS1.ws1_distinct_faces_atomless_not_recoverable` — genuine face-plurality on an atomless field is free
- `Series08.WS1.ws1_freeness_needs_two_positions` — freeness is a between-two-positions (distributed) phenomenon
- `Series08.WS1.ws1_plural_faces_free_witness` — the `|Q| = 2` free, non-symmetric witness (`labelLoop`)

- `Series08.WS2.ws2_monism_broken`

- `Series08.WS5.ws5_kill_condition` — now the DEPTH-ADVANCING witness (`pingPong`, `h ≠ h'`, breadth preserved; series-review-1 R1)

---

**Count: 38 headline theorems, all on the standard three.** The build is `sorry`-free and axiom-clean. `scripts/gate.sh` confirms Series 08 imports resolve only to `Series08.*` (+ Mathlib) — nothing from `series-07/`, `series-04/`, or `archive/`.
